properties.remark: THE C-MAN STATION AT SAND KEY LIGHT MEASURED A 35 KT GUST.
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A WATERSPOUT AT 316 PM LOCAL NEAR SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT. THE WATERSPOUT WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A CLOUD LINE THAT WAS SOUTH OF MARATHON AT THE TIME.

A WIND GUST OF 47.4 KNOTS...OR 54.5 MPH...FROM THE WEST WAS RECORDED AT THE NEW WEATHERFLOW MESONET STATION AT SMITH SHOAL LIGHT. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH THUNDERSTORMS IN AN EASTWARD-MOVING MESOSCALE CONVECTIVE SYSTEM.
ASSOCIATED WITH A WELL-DEFINED LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS MOVING EASTWARD CLOSE TO 30 MPH.
THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST AS A LINE OF SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS PASSED THE MIDDLE KEYS.
WIND GUSTS MEASURED AT LONG KEY LIGHT AS A LINE OF SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS PASSED.
THE C-MAN STATION AT MOLASSES REEF MEASURED A WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS...AS A LINE OF SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS PASSED BY THE STATION.

A WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS...OR 40 MPH...FROM THE WEST-NORTHWEST WAS MEASURED AT THE C-MAN STATION AT LONG KEY LIGHT. THIS WIND GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH AN EMBEDDED THUNDERSTORM IN A LINE OF SHOWERS AHEAD OF A TROUGH OF LOW PRESSURE.
NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE METEOROLOGISTS DETECTED A WATERSPOUT 2 MILES SOUTH OF KEY WEST. A SPRAY RING WAS NOT VISIBLE. THE WATERSPOUT LASTED NEARLY 15 MINUTES.
THE LONG KEY C-MAN STATION MEASURED A 36 KNOT WIND GUST FROM THE NORTH NORTHEAST. THE GUST WAS ASSOCIATED WITH THE PASSAGE OF A THUNDERSTORM.

THE SAND KEY C-MAN STATION MEASURED A 37 KNOT WIND GUST FROM THE NORTH NORTHEAST. THE STRONGEST CORE OF WINDS...ESTIMATED FROM RADAR...PASSED JUST SOUTHEAST OF THE STATION. THE ANEMOMETER HEIGHT IS 149 FEET ABOVE SEA LEVEL.
A CLUSTER OF THUNDERSTORMS IN THE AREA PRODUCED A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS AT THE LONG KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION AT 634 AM LOCAL.
36 KT WIND GUST ASSOCIATED WITH THUNDERSTORMS MOVING WEST OBSERVED AT THE SAND KEY C-MAN STATION.
35 KT WIND GUST ASSOCIATED WITH THUNDERSTORM OBSERVED AT THE SAND KEY C-MAN STATION.
38 KT WIND GUST ASSOCIATED WITH THUNDERSTORM WAS MEASURED AT MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION.
OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT SOUTH OF STOCK ISLAND. SPOUT WAS REPORTED AS ALL THE WAY DOWN TO THE SURFACE.
OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED A WATERSPOUT AT LEAST HALF WAY DOWN.
CHARTER BOAT CAPTAIN VICINITY MOSQUITO BANK REPORTED 1 WATERSPOUT ALL THE WAY TO THE SURFACE AND 1 FUNNEL CLOUD.
CHARTER BOAT CAPTAIN VICINITY OF MOSQUITO BANK CALLED BACK TO REPORT 3 WATERSPOUTS APPROXIMATELY 2 NAUTICAL MILES SOUTHEAST OF 25 05N AND 80 23W.
SPOTTER 200 REPORTED 2 WATERSPOUTS WITH ONE FULLY DOWN AND THE SECOND OCCASIONALLY TOUCHING SURFACE.
SPOTTER 200 CALLED IN WITH THE REPORT OF A THIN WATERSPOUT OUT IN THE VICINITY OF MOLASSES REEF LIGHT.
CHARTER BOAT CAPTAIN IN THE VICINITY OF SPIEGEL GROVE WRECK REPORTED A THIN WATERSPOUT IN THE VICINITY OF MOLASSES REEF LIGHT.

SPOTTER CALLED IN WITH REPORT OF 3 WATERSPOUTS...2 VERY BIG...ONE THIN...AND A POTENTIAL 4TH SPOUT OFF OF DUCK KEY. THESE ARE ASSOCIATED WITH A CLUSTER OF SHOWERS MOVING EAST. SPOTTER SAID THEY HAD JUST STARTED.
SPOTTER REPORTED 2 WATERSPOUTS IN NEARSHORE WATERS BETWEEN MARATHON AND EAST WASHERWOMAN SHOAL LIGHT. SPOUTS ASSOCIATED WITH A CLUSTER OF SHOWERS MOVING EAST NORTHEAST.

SEVERAL MEMBERS OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A LARGE...MATURE WATERSPOUT OVER THE NEARSHORE WATERS ALONG THE SOUTH SIDE OF KEY WEST. WATERSPOUT DURATION IS ESTIMATED AT 22 MINUTES...WITH MOVEMENT NEARLY STATIONARY FOR MOST OF THIS TIME. THE WATERSPOUT ATTAINED A SLOW WESTWARD MOTION TOWARD THE END OF ITS LIFE. THE SPOUT APPARENTLY APPROACHED WITHIN 300 YARDS OF SHORE...NEAR HIGGS BEACH.
PILOT REPORTED A WATERSPOUT OVER THE NEARSHORE GULF WATERS ASSOCIATED WITH A LINE OF SHOWERS WITH EMBEDDED THUNDERSTORMS MOVING NORTHEAST.
PILOT REPORTED 2 WATERSPOUTS OVER THE NEARSHORE ATLANTIC WATERS.
PILOT REPORTED 2 WATERSPOUTS OVER THE NEARSHORE ATLANTIC WATERS.

A SHORT-LIVED...WEAK WATERSPOUT...WITH NO SPRAY RING... WAS OBSERVED BY A NAVY OBSERVER. THE WATERSPOUT DEVELOPED IN ASSOCIATION WITH A GROWING CUMULUS CONGESTUS CLOUD LINE ALONG THE NORTH SIDE OF THE LOWER FLORIDA KEYS. ESTIMATED DURATION 2 MINUTES.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY AN OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE METEOROLOGIST. THE WATERSPOUT WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A CUMULUS CONGESTUS CLOUD LINE.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC NORTH OF KEY WEST. THE WATERSPOUT WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A CUMULUS CONGESTUS CLOUD LINE.
AN OBSERVER AT THE NAVAL AIR STATION BOCA CHICA REPORTED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES TO THE NORTH AND EAST OF BOCA CHICA KEY.

A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 8 NAUTICAL MILES NORTH OF THE SEVEN MILE BRIDGE. THE WATERSPOUT WAS ASSOCIATED WITH SHOWERS FORMING ALONG A CLOUD LINE NORTH OF US-1. DURATION UNKNOWN.
AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE SPOTTED SEVERAL WATER SPOUTS IN A CLOUD LINE ABOUT 3 MILES WNW OF THE KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT DURING THE EVENING OF MAY 29TH.

A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED ONE LARGE WATERSPOUT ALONG WITH TWO SMALLER FLANKING WATERSPOUTS. THE LARGEST WATERSPOUT WAS DESCRIBED AS AN ELEPHANT TRUNK AND HAD A WELL DEVELOPED SPRAY RING. THE TWO SMALLER WATERSPOUTS WERE DEVELOPING SPRAY RINGS.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A BRIEF WATERSPOUT IN BETWEEN CUDJOE AND SUMMERLAND KEY. A SPRAY RING WAS NOT VISIBLE DUE TO BLOCKAGE...AND THE FUNNEL WAS DESCRIBED AS THIN.
OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE SPOTTED A LARGE WATER SPOUT 5 MILES DUE SOUTH OF SHARK KEY.
WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC AND VERIFIED BY AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE ABOUT 1 TO 1.5 MILES SOUTH OF HIGGS BEACH.
THE SMITH SHOAL MESONET STATION MEASURED A 38 KNOT WIND GUST DURING A THUNDERSTORM.
THE SAND KEY C-MAN STATION MEASURED A 35 KNOT...WEST-SOUTHWESTERLY...WIND GUST WITH THE PASSING OF A THUNDERSTORM GUST FRONT.
THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION RECORDED A WIND GUST TO 39-KTS OUT OF THE WEST SOUTHWEST AS A LINE OF SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS MOVED THROUGH THE AREA.
THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN WEATHER STATION RECORDED A WIND GUST TO 34 KNOTS AT 312 PM LOCAL AS SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS IN ASSOCIATION WITH A SQUALL LINE MOVED THROUGH THE AREA.
THE MOLASSES REEF LIGHT C-MAN WEATHER STATION REPORTED A 36 KNOT WIND GUST OUT OF THE SOUTH AT 429 PM LOCAL AS THUNDERSTORMS MOVED OVER THE AREA.
A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED A THIN WATERSPOUT JUST SOUTH OF CURRY HAMMOCK STATE PARK...ON THE EAST END OF MARATHON. A SPRAY RING WAS NOT CONFIRMED DUE TO VISIBLE OBSTRUCTION.

AN OFF DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE...AND A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER...REPORTED A WATERSPOUT JUST SOUTHEAST OF THE WEST END OF MARATHON. THE TRAINED SPOTTER PROCEEDED ON FOOT TO GET A BETTER VIEW OF THE BASE AND SAW A SPRAY RING. BOTH DESCRIBED THE FUNNEL AS THIN.
MONROE COUNTY SHERIFF DISPATCH AND ISLAMORADA FIRE AND RESCUE REPORTED TWO WATERSPOUTS OVER FLORIDA BAY JUST OFFSHORE ROCK HARBOR AT 751 PM LOCAL. MOVEMENT WAS TOWARDS THE EAST AT AROUND 10 MPH.

AN OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE AS WELL A STORM SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 4 MILES SOUTH OF KEY LARGO AT 821 PM LOCAL. THE WATERSPOUT WAS SIGHTED ALONG THE EDGE OF A SMALL CLUSTER OF SHOWERS MOVING THROUGH THE AREA AT ABOUT 10 MPH.

TRAINED SPOTTER 228 REPORTED A LARGE WATERSPOUT ESTIMATED TO BE MOVING NORTH WITH AN ESTIMATED DURATION OF 7 MINUTES. WATERSPOUT WAS LOST TO SIGHT DUE TO GROUND OBSTRUCTIONS AND HAD DISSIPATED BY THE TIME SPOTTER MOVED TO AREA WITH CLEAR HORIZON.
A STRONG THUNDERSTORM BEHIND AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY GENERATED A WIND GUST OF 51 KNOTS AT THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION PLATFORM.

TWO PUBLIC REPORTS...AND ONE FROM A TRAINED TOWER OBSERVER AT THE NAS NQX...CONFIRMED A WATERSPOUT JUST NORTH OF KEY HAVEN. THE SPOUT HAD AN ELEPHANT TRUNK-LIKE FUNNEL AND A SPRAY RING. MOVEMENT WAS DESCRIBED AS NEARLY STATIONARY.
DURING A PASSING THUNDERSTORM...THE SAND KEY C-MAN STATION MEASURED A PEAK WIND GUST OF 40 KNOTS.
THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 35 KNOTS.
A 42 KT WIND GUST FROM THE SSE WAS REPORTED BY THE SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION.
WEATHERFLOW MESONET STATION AT SMITH SHOAL MEASURED A 36 KT WIND GUST FROM THE SOUTHEAST ASSOCIATED WITH A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS MOVING NORTH.
A WIND GUST OF 48 KTS WAS REPORTED BY THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION BY A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS MOVING NORTH TO NORTHEAST AT 40 KTS.
A 37 KT WIND GUST FROM THE SOUTHWEST WAS REPORTED BY THE MOLASSES REEF LIGHT C-MAN STATION ASSOCIATED WITH A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS MOVING NORTHEAST AT 30 KTS.
A SOUTHWEST 39 KT WIND GUST ASSOCIATED WITH A NORTHEAST MOVING LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS WAS REPORTED AT FORT JEFFERSON IN THE DRY TORTUGAS NATIONAL PARK.
WEATHERFLOW MESONET STATION AT SMITH SHOAL MEASURED A 38 KT WIND GUST FROM THE SOUTHWEST ASSOCIATED WITH A LINE OF SHOWERS WITH SOME EMBEDDED THUNDERSTORMS MOVING NORTHEAST.
SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION REPORTED A SOUTHWEST WIND GUST OF 47 KTS ASSOCIATED WITH A FAST MOVING LINE OF SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS.
WEATHERFLOW MESONET STATION AT SMITH SHOAL REPORTED A 43 KT WIND GUST FROM THE SOUTHWEST ASSOCIATED WITH A LINE OF RAPIDLY MOVING SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS.
THE ASOS AT KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT REPORTED A 39 KT WIND GUST FROM THE WEST SOUTHWEST ASSOCIATED WITH A FAST MOVING LINE OF SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS.
A 37 KT WIND GUST FROM THE SOUTHWEST WAS REPORTED BY THE HANDAR WITHIN THE KEY DEER NATIONAL WILDLIFE REFUGE ON BIG PINE KEY,
A WIND GUST OF 39 KTS FROM THE SOUTH SOUTHWEST WAS REPORTED BY THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION ASSOCIATED WITH A FAST MOVING LINE OF SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS.

A CHARTER CAPTAIN REPORTED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 1.5 MILES NORTH OF LOOE KEY LIGHT AT 719 PM LOCAL. THE WATERSPOUT WAS SLOW MOVING AND ASSOCIATED WITH SHOWERS ALONG A CLOUD LINE SOUTH OF THE LOWER KEYS.

A WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED FROM THE FOUR-STORY UPPER AIR TOWER LOCATED AT THE WFO IN KEY WEST. LOCATION WAS ESTIMATED TO BE 10 MILES EAST NORTHEAST OF KEY WEST...AND VERIFIED FROM RADAR. THE FUNNEL WAS FAIRLY THICK...MOVEMENT WAS NEARLY STATIONARY...AND DURATION WAS LESS THAN FIVE MINUTES.

A BOATER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT SOUTH OF MOLASSES REEF. FROM RADAR OBSERVATIONS...THE CELL WAS 5 MILES SOUTH OF MOLASSES REEF AND WAS DRIFTING WEST NORTHWEST AT 7 KNOTS. A SPRAY RING WAS NOT VISIBLE AND THE SPOUT LASTED 12 MINUTES. THE CALLER ALSO NOTICED THAT THE PARENT CELL DISSIPATED QUICKLY.
THE WEATHERFLOW MESONET STATION REPORTED A WIND GUST TO 35 KNOTS AT 645 PM LOCAL AS A LINE OF SHOWERS AND THUNDERSTORMS MOVED OVER THE AREA.
CMAN MEASURED A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 42 KNOTS.
CMAN MEASURED A THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST OF 38 KNOTS.
MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION RECORDED A 37 KNOT WIND GUST AT 0356 AM THAT WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A THUNDERSTORM IN THE AREA.
SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION RECORDED A 36 KNOT WIND GUST AT 948 PM EDT.
A SHORT LIVED THUNDERSTORM COLLAPSED AND TRIGGERED AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY WHICH PASSED THROUGH SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION PLATFORM WITH A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS OR 41 MPH.
THE MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION MEASURED A 35 KNOT WIND GUST...DIRECTLY UNDERNEATH THE CORE OF A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
A WIND GUST OF 38 KNOTS WAS MEASURED BY THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION.
WATERSPOUT CALLED IN BY PUBLIC AND CO-OP OBSERVER AT 301PM, IN KEMP CHANNEL, ABOUT 2 MILES NORTH OF CUDJOE KEY. ESTIMATED DURATION AROUND 15 MINUTES.
AN EAST-SOUTHEAST WIND GUST OF 38 KTS WAS REPORTED BY THE SAND KEY LIGHT CMAN STATION ASSOCIATED WITH A CLUSTER OF SHOWERS WITH EMBEDDED THUNDERSTORMS.
A LINE OF STRONG THUNDERSTORMS MOVING TOWARDS THE NORTH AND WEST PRODUCED A WIND GUST TO 38 KNOTS AT THE SAND KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION AT 814 AM LOCAL.
THE SMITH SHOAL LIGHT WEATHERFLOW STATION RECORDED A WIND GUST TO 37 KNOTS OUT OF THE SOUTHEAST AT 830 AM AS A THUNDERSTORM PASSED OVER THE SITE.
THE MOLASSES REEF LIGHT C-MAN STATION RECORDED A WIND GUST TO 36 KNOTS AT 957 AM LOCAL AS A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS MOVED OVER THE REEF.
THE UNIVERSITY OF SOUTH FLORIDA COMPS AUTOMATED STATION SOUTH OF CAPE SABLE RECORDED A WIND GUST TO 39 KNOTS AS A LINE OF THUNDERSTORMS MOVED OVER NORTHWEST FLORIDA BAY.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE PERSONNEL SPOTTED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES TO THE NORTH-NORTHEAST OF KEY WEST AT APPROXIMATELY 210 PM LOCAL. THE THIN ROPE-LIKE WATERSPOUT LASTED APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES BEFORE DISAPPATING.
WEATHER OBSERVERS FROM THE BOCA CHICA NAVAL AIR STATION REPORTED A THIN WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY 6 MILES TO THE NORTH-NORTHEAST OF BOCA CHICA AT 237 PM LOCAL. ITS DURATION WAS LESS THAN 10 MINUTES.

DELAYED REPORT THROUGH SOCIAL MEDIA...A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED ABOUT ONE NAUTICAL MILE NORTHEAST OF BOCA GRANDE KEY...IN LAKES PASSAGE. A SPRAY RING AND A THIN FUNNEL WERE OBSERVED ON THE PHOTOGRAPH. DURATION UNKNOWN.
THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION RECENTLY MEASURED A 39 KNOT WIND GUST WITH A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
THE SMITH SHOAL LIGHT MESONET MEASURED A 31 KNOT WIND GUST FROM A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
THE SMITH SHOAL LIGHT MESONET MEASURED A 36 KNOT WIND GUST FROM A PASSING THUNDERSTORM.
WIND GUST OF 38 KNOTS WAS REPORTED BY THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION
WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS WAS REPORTED AT LONG KEY LIGHT DUE TO OUTFLOW FROM DECAYING THUNDERSTORMS.
AN ADDITIONAL WIND GUST OF 38 KNOTS WAS MEASURED BY THE LONG KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION.
A WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS WAS MEASURED AT THE SMITH SHOAL LIGHT CO OP SITE AS THE OUTFLOW FROM ADVANCING THUNDERSTORMS PASSED OVERHEAD.
PASSING THUNDERSTORMS PRODUCED A WIND GUST OF 36 KNOTS...41 MPH...AS IT PASSED ACROSS SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT.

SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 53 KNOTS.

SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION REPOTED A 50 KNOT WIND GUST.
WEATHERFLOW STATION AT SMITH SHOAL RECORDED A 45 KNOT WIND GUST.

WEATHERFLOW STATION AT SMITH SHOAL RECORDED A 40 KNOT WIND GUST.
SAND KEY C-MAN STATION RECORDED A 45 KNOT WIND GUST.

A TRAINED SPOTTER OBSERVED A MATURE WATERSPOUT WITH A SPRAY RING ABOUT 2 MILES NORTH OF THE 33RD STREET BOAT RAMP IN MARATHON. MEDIUM FUNNEL WIDTH. INITIAL MOVEMENT WAS TOWARD THE WEST AT LESS THAN 5 KNOTS. TOTAL DURATION 16 MINUTES.

A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED TWO THIN ROPE-LIKE WATERSPOUTS JUST SOUTH AND WEST OF GEIGER KEY IN THE LOWER KEYS. MOVEMENT WAS SLOW TOWARDS THE SOUTHWEST AND THEIR DURATION WAS LESS THAN 10 MINUTES.

TWO WATERSPOUTS REPORTED BY AN OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE. HORIZON COULD NOT BE SEEN FOR EITHER WATERSPOUT. ONE WATERSPOUT WAS A STRAIGHT SKINNY ROPE...REACHED NEARLY TO THE HORIZON...AND HAD A LIGHT GREY-BLUE COLOR. THE SECOND WATERSPOUT WAS UPSTREAM FROM THE MATURE WATERSPOUT AND WAS ABOUT 1/4 THE WAY DOWN WITH A DARK BLUE COLOR. THE FIRST WATERSPOUT HAD A DURATION OF 15 MINUTES, WHILE THE SECOND WATERSPOUT ONLY LASTED ABOUT 5 MINUTES. BOTH WATERSPOUTS WERE MOVING TOWARDS THE NW AT ABOUT 10 MPH.

WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY THE USCG. THE OBSERVER COULD SEE THE HORIZION, BUT NO SPRAY RING AT THE SURFACE. THE WATERSPOUT WAS ROPE SHAPED, TILTED 30 DEGREES, AND GREY. THERE WAS NO THUNDER/LIGHTNING JUST A LIGHT RAIN SHAFT. THE WATERSPOUT APPEARED STATIONARY TO THE OBSERVER AND WAS EPISODIC.
OBSERVERS AT KEY WEST INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT SPOTTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 5 MILES SOUTHEAST OF KEY WEST. DURATION 4 MINUTES.

NWS EMPLOYEE CALLED IN A WATERSPOUT REPORT SOUTH OF SUGARLOAF. THE OBSERVER COULD NOT SEE THE HORIZON. THE WATERSPOUT APPERARED TO BE OF A MEDIUM THINCKNESS AND GROWING. HE DID NOT SEE A RAIN SHAFT OR LIGHTNING AND DID NOT HEAR THUNDER. THE SPOUT WAS VERTICAL WITH NO TILT AND WAS DARK BLUE. THE OBSERVER COULD NOT DETERMINE MOVEMENT OR DISSAPATION TIME. HE DID DETERMINE THAT HE COULD SEE IT FOR AT LEAST 10 MINUTES.
PILOT REPORT OF NUMEROUS WATERSPOUTS.
FIREFIGHTER CALLED AND REPORTED A WATERSPOUT JUST 1 MILE NORTH OF THE OLD SEVEN MILE BRIDGE.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 1 MILE NORTH OF SUGARLOAF LODGE ON SUGARLOAF KEY. DURATION UNKNOWN.
THE RAMROD KEY CW0924 MEASURED A 34 KNOT WIND GUST FROM THE OUTFLOW OF A NEARBY THUNDERSTORM.
THE CUDJOE KEY CW0925 MEASURED A 35 KNOT WIND GUST FROM THE OUTFLOW OF A NEARBY THUNDERSTORM.
A SHORT-LIVED FUNNEL WAS OBSERVED BY A TRAINED SPOTTER OVER FLORIDA BAY JUST NORTH OF LOWER MATECUMBE KEY. NO SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE.
THE WIND ALERT STATION IN MARATHON...AT VALHALLA BEACH RESORT...MEASURED A 36 KNOT WIND GUST DURING A RECENT THUNDERSTORM.

MULTIPLE OFF-DUTY NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE EMPLOYEES WITNESSED 3 WATERSPOUTS APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES SOUTH OF THE NAVAL AIR STATION ON BOCA CHICA KEY. THE THIN ROPE-LIKE WATERSPOUTS DEVELOPED IN BETWEEN TWO SEPERATE RAIN SHIELDS AND EACH LASTED APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES BEFORE DISAPPATING.

SAILOR JUST WEST OF MULE KEY REPORTED A MATURE WATERSPOUT WITH A MEDIUM THICKNES, A STRIAGHT UP-DOWN AXIS, AND A VISIBLE SPRAY RING. THERE WAS A RAIN SHAFT FOLLOWING THE WATERSPOUT AND THUNDER AND LIGHTNING ASSOCIATED WITH THE EVENT. THE WATERSPOUT APPEARED TO BE MOVING TOWARDS THE WEST AT 10 MPH AND IT PASSED JUST NORTH OF MULE KEY. THE DURATION OF THE WATERSPOUT WAS 15 MINUTES.

MULTIPLE FUNNELS SIGHTED ALONG THE KEY WEST COAST. A WATERSPOUT WAS SPOTTED FROM TIME OF FORMATION TILL TIME OF DECAY BY A NWS EMPLOYEE. THE WATERSPOUT WAS LOCATED ABOUT 120 YARDS FROM THE S COAST OF KEY WEST. THE WATERSPOUT DEVELOPED INTO A LARGE MATURE WATERSPOUT WHICH APPEARED TO BE A DARK BLUE COLOR. IT WAS FOLLOWED BY A LARGE RAIN SHAFT. THERE WAS NO THUNDER OR LIGHTNING ASSOCIATED WITH THE EVENT. THE WATERSPOUT WAS MOVING TOWARD THE SE AT ABOUT 5 MPH. DURATION WAS ABOUT 20 MINUTES.

SAILOR JUST WEST OF MULE KEY REPORTED A MATURE WATERSPOUT WITH A MEDIUM THICKNESS, A STRIAGHT UP-DOWN AXIS, AND A VISIBLE SPRAY RING. THERE WAS A RAIN SHAFT FOLLOWING THE WATERSPOUT AND THUNDER AND LIGHTNING ASSOCIATED WITH THE EVENT. THE WATERSPOUT APPEARED TO BE MOVING TOWARDS THE WEST AT 10 MPH AND IT PASSED JUST NORTH OF MULE KEY. THE DURATION OF THE WATERSPOUT WAS 15 MINUTES.
AIRPLANE PILOT REPORT OF MULTIPLE WATERSPOUTS AND FUNNELS 4 MILES NORTH OF KEY WEST.

A TRAINED SPOTTER ON CUDJOE KEY REPORTED A WATERSPOUT ABOUT 3 MILES NORTH OF SUGARLOAF KEY AT 205 PM LOCAL. A THICK CONDENSATION FUNNEL WAS OBSERVED, HOWEVER, NO SPRAY RING WAS VISIBLE DUE TO THE OBSERVERS LOCATION. DURATION WAS APPROXIMATELY 10 MINUTES.
A WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED BY A FIRE DEPARTMENT EMPLOYEE IN HAWK CHANNEL.

MATURE WATERSPOUT REPORTED BY A SHIP LOCATED 11 MILES OFFSHORE TO THE WNW OF KEY WEST. SPRAY RING AND RAIN SHAFT WERE VISIBLE. WATERSPOUT WAS MOVING TOWARD THE SW. FIRST OBSERVED REPORT OF THE WATERSPOUT WAS REPORTED JUST AFTER 6 PM.
THE MOLASSES REEF C-MAN STATION REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 36 KTS ASSOCIATED WITH A FAST-MOVING CLUSTER OF SHOWERS.
DAVIS STATION LOCATED AT THE FLORIDA KEYS ACQUEDUCT AUTHORITY PUMPING STATION ON LONG KEY REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 34 KTS ASSOCIATED WITH A FAST MOVING CLUSTER OF SHOWERS.
A CHARTER CAPTAIN REPORTED A WATERSPOUT APPROXIMATELY TWO MILES SOUTH OF BOCA CHICA WITHIN A CLOUD LINE SOUTH OF THE ISLAND CHAIN. SIZE AND DURATION UNKNOWN.
THE MOLASSES REEF LIGHT C-MAN STATION RECORDED A WIND GUST TO 41-KNOTS OUT OF THE NORTHEAST AT 549PM LOCAL AS SHOWERS MOVED THROUGH.
THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION RECORDED A WIND GUST OF 39 KNOTS OUT OF THE NORTHEAST AT 746PM LOCAL AS SHOWERS MOVED OVER THE REEF.
THE MOLASSES REEF LIGHT C-MAN STATION MEASURED A WIND GUST TO 37-KNOTS OUT OF THE NORTHEAST AS A LINE OF SHOWERS MOVED THROUGH.
THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION MEASURED A 40-KNOT WIND GUST OUT OF THE EAST NORTH-EAST AT 1051PM LOCAL AS A LINE OF SHOWERS WAS PASSING OVER THE COASTAL WATERS SOUTH OF THE MIDDLE KEYS.
THE WEATHERFLOW STATION AT SMITH SHOAL LIGHT RECORDED A 34-KNOT WIND GUST OUT OF THE EAST NORTHEAST AT 1215AM LOCAL AS AN OUTFLOW BOUNDARY PUSHED ACROSS THE AREA.
THE LONG KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 34 KNOTS...OR 39 MPH.
THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION MEASURED A WIND GUST OF 35-KNOTS...OR 40 MPH.
THE SOMBRERO KEY LIGHT C-MAN STATION MEASURED A WIND GUST OF 43 KNOTS...OR 49 MPH.

A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED TWO THIN WATERSPOUTS ABOUT 1 MILE SOUTH OF MARATHON AROUND 537 PM LOCAL. THE WATERSPOUTS HAD LITTLE IN THE WAY OF TILT AND WERE LOCATED WITHIN A NARROW BAND OF NORTHWARD MOVING SHOWERS. MOVEMENT WAS TOWARDS THE NORTH BETWEEN 10 AND 15 MPH AND THEIR DURATION UNKNOWN.
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC REPORTED A THIN WATERSPOUT ABOUT 5 MILES SOUTH OF SILVER SHORES, NEAR MILE MARKER 96. DURATION UNKNOWN.
